_meta.description: Research Pipeline Chatflow — D-031 Phase A (controller-side fallback) + Phase B stub (enhanced-worker dispatch)
_meta.phase_a: Controller KI node handles /v1/search directly. No enhanced-workers required. KI_BASE_URL defaults to http://knowledge-index.ai-stack:8100.
_meta.phase_b: Set KI_BASE_URL env/variable to the target enhanced-worker KI address (http://<node>:8100) when dispatching research to a worker with web_search capability.
_meta.import_instructions: Import via Flowise UI: Chatflows > Add > Import. Or via API: POST /api/v1/chatflows/importchatflows. Credential 'litellm_openai_key' must be pre-configured in Flowise with the LiteLLM master key. KI_BASE_URL and KI_API_KEY are read from process.env at execution time.
_meta.credential_note: Create a Flowise OpenAI API credential named 'litellm_openai_key' pointing at http://litellm.ai-stack:4000 with the LiteLLM master key value.

data.label: LiteLLM Synthesis Model
data.name: chatOpenAI
data.description: OpenAI-compatible chat model via LiteLLM internal endpoint

inputParams.label: Base Path
inputParams.name: basePath
inputParams.description: Override the base URL for the OpenAI API

data.label: KI Web Search
data.name: customTool
data.description: HTTP tool: calls POST /v1/search on the Knowledge Index Service. Phase A uses controller KI; Phase B uses enhanced-worker KI (set KI_BASE_URL env).

inputParams.label: Input Schema
inputParams.name: schema
inputParams.description: Define the input schema for the tool

inputs.name: ki_web_search
inputs.description: Search the web for current information on a topic and ingest results into the research knowledge base. Use this when the user asks to research, investigate, or find current information about a topic. Returns a summary of ingested results.

schema.description: The research query to search for
Qdrant collection to store results in (default: web_research)

data.label: KI Knowledge Query
data.name: customTool
data.description: HTTP tool: calls POST /query on the Knowledge Index Service to retrieve relevant chunks from an ingested collection.

inputParams.label: Input Schema
inputParams.name: schema
inputParams.description: Define the input schema for the tool

inputs.name: ki_query_knowledge
inputs.description: Retrieve relevant information from the knowledge base that has already been ingested. Use after ki_web_search to pull the most relevant text chunks for answering the user's question. Returns ranked context passages with source citations.

schema.description: The query to search the knowledge base for

data.label: Research Supervisor
data.name: toolAgent
data.description: Agent that orchestrates web research and knowledge retrieval using KI tools. D-031 Phase A controller-side implementation.

inputs.systemMessage:
You are a research supervisor agent. Research topics thoroughly and provide well-sourced answers.

Research protocol (Phase A — controller node):
1. Call ki_web_search to fetch and ingest current information on the topic.
2. Call ki_query_knowledge to retrieve the most relevant passages from what was ingested.
3. Synthesize a clear, accurate answer grounded in the retrieved context.
4. Always cite sources (URLs) when available.
5. If ki_web_search returns 'unavailable' (no TAVILY_API_KEY on this node), skip to
   ki_query_knowledge to check for previously ingested information, then inform
   the user if no relevant context exists.

Phase B extension (enhanced-worker dispatch):
  The KI_BASE_URL environment variable will be set to the target enhanced-worker's
  KI endpoint when the controller dispatches research to a capable worker node.
  Both tools automatically use this override — no flow changes required.
